Codex Instruction: Inspect the existing repo structure and identify where a custom Gymnasium environment should live without forcing a new layout.
Acceptance Criteria: A short note exists in implementation summary explaining chosen file locations.

Codex Instruction: Check pyproject/requirements and add only minimal dependencies needed for gymnasium env, numpy, pygame, pytest, and optional stable-baselines3 checker.
Acceptance Criteria: Dependencies are minimal and importable; no heavy simulator/game engine added.
Notes: If SB3 is already absent, add it only if project allows it; otherwise make checker optional.

Codex Instruction: Create or update a lightweight implementation notes file listing assumptions and commands.
Acceptance Criteria: Notes include env id, commands, and known limitations.

Codex Instruction: Create the DroneIntercept3DEnv package/module structure fitting the repo style.
Acceptance Criteria: Environment imports cleanly from package.
Notes: Use existing package if one exists.

Codex Instruction: Add a dataclass for all tunable parameters: world size, speeds, accel, dt, capture radius, max steps, reward weights, render size.
Acceptance Criteria: All major knobs are editable from one config object.
Verification Command:
python - <<'PY'
from drone_env.envs.drone_intercept_3d import DroneIntercept3DConfig
print(DroneIntercept3DConfig())
PY
Notes: No magic constants scattered through step().

Codex Instruction: Implement continuous 3D acceleration action space Box(-1, 1, shape=(3,)).
Acceptance Criteria: action_space shape is (3,), dtype float32, bounded [-1, 1].
Verification Command:
python - <<'PY'
from drone_env.envs.drone_intercept_3d import DroneIntercept3DEnv
env=DroneIntercept3DEnv(); print(env.action_space)
PY

Codex Instruction: Implement 20-dimensional normalized observation vector with pursuer/target pos/vel, relative pos/vel, distance, previous distance.
Acceptance Criteria: Observation shape is (20,), finite, float32.

Codex Instruction: Register DroneIntercept3D-v0 with Gymnasium so gym.make can instantiate it.
Acceptance Criteria: gymnasium.make('DroneIntercept3D-v0') works after importing package.
Verification Command:
python - <<'PY'
import gymnasium as gym, drone_env
env=gym.make('DroneIntercept3D-v0')
print(env)
PY
Notes: Avoid duplicate registration crash on repeated imports.

Codex Instruction: Use Gymnasium-compatible seeding through reset(seed=...).
Acceptance Criteria: Same seed gives same initial state.

Codex Instruction: Spawn pursuer within world bounds, above floor, away from target.
Acceptance Criteria: Pursuer z > safe_min_z and within bounds.

Codex Instruction: Spawn target within world bounds and separated from pursuer by min initial distance.
Acceptance Criteria: Initial distance > capture_radius * 3 by default.

Codex Instruction: Store previous distance and previous action correctly for reward shaping.
Acceptance Criteria: previous_distance initialized to current distance and previous_action initialized to zeros.

Codex Instruction: Update pursuer velocity and position from clipped acceleration action using dt and max_accel.
Acceptance Criteria: Manual one-step calculation matches expected position/velocity.

Codex Instruction: Limit pursuer velocity norm to pursuer_max_speed.
Acceptance Criteria: Velocity norm never exceeds max speed by more than epsilon.

Codex Instruction: Add helper functions for distance, unit vector, speed clipping, in-bounds checks, and safe normalization.
Acceptance Criteria: Geometry tests pass and avoid division by zero.

Codex Instruction: Target moves with constant velocity and turns/bounces near boundaries.
Acceptance Criteria: Target remains in bounds during straight-mode rollout.

Codex Instruction: Target moves away from pursuer with small lateral/random motion while avoiding boundaries.
Acceptance Criteria: Target velocity finite, clipped, and generally points away when close.

Codex Instruction: Target circles around a center with slight altitude variation for visualization/debugging.
Acceptance Criteria: Orbit-mode rollout stays bounded and visibly changes x/y/z.

Codex Instruction: Return info['reward_breakdown'] with exactly the 10 named reward components even before tuning.
Acceptance Criteria: All 10 keys exist every step.

Codex Instruction: Add +100 capture reward when distance <= capture_radius.
Acceptance Criteria: Capture reward positive and episode terminates.

Codex Instruction: Add clipped progress reward: 4.0 * clip(prev_dist - curr_dist, -5, 5).
Acceptance Criteria: Progress reward positive when closer and negative when farther.

Codex Instruction: Add -0.03 * current_distance penalty.
Acceptance Criteria: Distance penalty is negative and proportional to distance.

Codex Instruction: Add -0.05 per-step penalty.
Acceptance Criteria: Every non-terminal normal step includes time_penalty = -0.05.

Codex Instruction: Reward cosine similarity between pursuer velocity and direction to target, clamped [-1.5, 1.5].
Acceptance Criteria: Alignment reward positive when moving toward target, negative when moving away.
Notes: Use safe norm for zero velocity.

Codex Instruction: Reward actual closing speed using relative velocity projected onto target direction, clamped to reasonable bounds.
Acceptance Criteria: Closing speed reward positive when relative motion closes distance.

Codex Instruction: Penalize squared action magnitude using -0.02 * ||action||^2.
Acceptance Criteria: Zero action has no energy penalty; max action has negative penalty.

Codex Instruction: Penalize action change using -0.01 * ||action - previous_action||^2.
Acceptance Criteria: Penalty increases with larger action changes.

Codex Instruction: Penalize z below safe_min_z or above safe_max_z.
Acceptance Criteria: Penalty is zero in safe band and negative outside it.

Codex Instruction: Add -100 crash penalty and -75 out-of-bounds penalty under one breakdown key.
Acceptance Criteria: Crash/out-of-bounds produce negative safety_failure_penalty.

Codex Instruction: Terminate when distance <= capture_radius and set info['captured']=True.
Acceptance Criteria: Capture test passes.

Codex Instruction: Terminate when pursuer z <= 0 and set info['crashed']=True.
Acceptance Criteria: Crash test passes.

Codex Instruction: Terminate when pursuer leaves arena beyond margin and set info['out_of_bounds']=True.
Acceptance Criteria: Out-of-bounds test passes.
Notes: Target should avoid bounds, pursuer can fail bounds.

Codex Instruction: Truncate at max_steps without marking terminated unless also captured/crashed/OOB.
Acceptance Criteria: truncated=True at max_steps.

Codex Instruction: Create a lightweight pygame renderer with fixed isometric 3D-to-2D projection.
Acceptance Criteria: Renderer initializes without opening multiple duplicate windows.

Codex Instruction: Render arena bounds and a simple floor grid so 3D movement is understandable.
Acceptance Criteria: Human render clearly shows arena and floor plane.

Codex Instruction: Render pursuer, target, distance line, and short trails for both drones.
Acceptance Criteria: Both drones and trails are visible during rollout.

Codex Instruction: Render step count, distance, reward, and captured/crashed flags.
Acceptance Criteria: HUD text visible and updates each frame.

Codex Instruction: Implement render_mode='rgb_array' returning H x W x 3 numpy array.
Acceptance Criteria: rgb_array test passes.
Notes: If impossible, skip test with clear reason, but attempt first.

Codex Instruction: Implement close() to quit pygame resources.
Acceptance Criteria: Repeated env create/render/close does not crash.

Codex Instruction: Create scripts/check_env.py using SB3 check_env if available, with clear fallback message if SB3 is absent.
Acceptance Criteria: Script exits 0 when env is valid.

Codex Instruction: Create scripts/random_rollout.py with args for episodes, target mode, render mode, seed.
Acceptance Criteria: Prints reward, length, captured, final distance per episode.

Codex Instruction: Create scripts/manual_policy_demo.py with direction-to-target hand-coded pursuit policy.
Acceptance Criteria: In straight mode, final distance usually improves or intercepts.

Codex Instruction: Create optional scripts/reward_debug.py that prints reward breakdown over a short manual rollout.
Acceptance Criteria: Reward breakdown columns print cleanly.

Codex Instruction: Add pytest tests for reset and step Gymnasium API correctness.
Acceptance Criteria: Reset/step tests pass.

Codex Instruction: Add pytest tests for capture, crash, OOB, and timeout behavior.
Acceptance Criteria: Termination tests pass.
Verification Command: pytest -q tests/test_drone_intercept_3d.py -k 'capture or crash or out_of_bounds or timeout'

Codex Instruction: Add pytest tests for all 10 reward components and progress sign.
Acceptance Criteria: Reward tests pass.
Verification Command: pytest -q tests/test_drone_intercept_3d.py -k 'reward or penalty or alignment or closing'

Codex Instruction: Add pytest tests for rgb_array and close/reopen behavior, skipped only if renderer unavailable in CI.
Acceptance Criteria: Rendering tests pass or skip with explicit reason.
Notes: Headless environments may need SDL dummy driver.

Codex Instruction: Run the entire test suite and fix failures without weakening tests.
Acceptance Criteria: All tests pass or failures are clearly documented if external dependency issue.
Notes: Do not claim success unless run.

Codex Instruction: Run scripts/check_env.py and fix Gymnasium/SB3 compatibility issues.
Acceptance Criteria: check_env passes or absence of SB3 is documented with equivalent API tests passing.

Codex Instruction: Run random rollout for at least 3 episodes to verify no crashes.
Acceptance Criteria: Script completes and prints episode stats.

Codex Instruction: Run manual policy in straight mode without rendering and verify distance generally improves.
Acceptance Criteria: Manual policy improves distance or captures in straight mode.

Codex Instruction: Run one short human-render demo and verify visualization works.
Acceptance Criteria: Window opens, renders drones, closes cleanly.

Codex Instruction: Summarize changed files, commands run, results, assumptions, and TODOs.
Acceptance Criteria: Summary is truthful and includes command outputs or status.
